goal: Nail exact timing and cadence for the 75-second intro.
Practice 18-minute behavioral panel with concise STARs and rapid transitions.
Practice 42-minute coding mocks with a plan→implement→test rhythm.
Simulate back-to-back rehearsals: 18-minute panel → short reset → 42-minute coding.
4-box system design drill with high-level clarity and one-sentence tradeoffs per box.
Go idioms & Kubernetes ops questions to reduce friction in implementation and system answers.
Filler-pruning: prune fillers and craft concise hooks for STARs.
Starter session 1: Quick behavioral + filler prune (45m).
Weekly time plan: 6 evenings + 1 weekend block (approx).
Provide a concrete STAR-based example for a technical leader with hook and timed segments (S/T/A/R) for ~60–75s.
Provide metric-focused guidance for backend interview responses, including categories and ready-to-use templates.
Incorporate hostile/mock sessions into the practice plan to build resilience.
Improve speed at reading unfamiliar code snippets during debugging tasks.
entities: priority_items: 75s intro; 18m behavioral; 42m coding; back-to-back rehearsals; 4-box design; Go/Kubernetes; filler pruning; starter sessions
starter_sessions: two starter sessions (behavioral + filler prune; coding pattern rebuild)
STAR_coaching_entity: concrete example for technical leader STAR structure with hook and timing
metrics_catalog: latency (p50/p90/p95/p99), throughput (QPS), error rate, availability, MTTR/MTTD, cost efficiency, deployment & lead time, data/storage, capacity/scaling
metrics_templates: ready-to-use phrasing templates and STAR lines for backend metrics
behavioral_red_flags_checklist: common red flags and fixes to avoid in behavioral STARs
hostile_mock_plan: base layer 70–80% regular mocks; stress layer 20–30%; 2 harsh mocks in Weeks 9–11; ground rules; back-to-back panel→coding; debrief structure
hostile_mock_scripts_examples: sample interruption phrases and debrief prompts to use by mock interviewer (e.g., “That’s not detailed enough”, “Why would that scale?”, etc.)
open: Confirm whether to write the 75-second intro word-for-word.
